\"meta\": null}], \"structuredContent\": {\"content\": \"1: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\\n2:  * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\\n3:  * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\\n4: \\n5: use std::collections::HashMap;\\n6: use std::sync::Arc;\\n7: \\n8: use base::id::PainterId;\\n9: use embedder_traits::UntrustedNodeAddress;\\n10: use euclid::Size2D;\\n11: use fonts::FontContext;\\n12: use layout_api::wrapper_traits::ThreadSafeLayoutNode;\\n13: use layout_api::{\\n14:     AnimatingImages, IFrameSizes, LayoutImageDestination, PendingImage, PendingImageState,\\n15:     PendingRasterizationImage,\\n16: };\\n17: use net_traits::image_cache::{\\n18:     Image as CachedImage, ImageCache, ImageCacheResult, ImageOrMetadataAvailable, PendingImageId,\\n19: };\\n20: use parking_lot::{Mutex, RwLock};\\n21: use pixels::RasterImage;\\n22: use script::layout_dom::ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode;\\n23: use servo_url::{ImmutableOrigin, ServoUrl};\\n24: use style::context::SharedStyleContext;\\n25: use style::dom::OpaqueNode;\\n26: use style::values::computed::image::{Gradient, Image};\\n27: use webrender_api::units::{DeviceIntSize, DeviceSize};\\n28: \\n29: pub(crate) type CachedImageOrError = Result<CachedImage, ResolveImageError>;\\n30: \\n31: pub(crate) struct LayoutContext<'a> {\\n32:     pub use_rayon: bool,\\n33: \\n34:     /// Bits shared by the layout and style system.\\n35:     pub style_context: SharedStyleContext<'a>,\\n36: \\n37:     /// A FontContext to be used during layout.\\n38:     pub font_context: Arc<FontContext>,\\n39: \\n40:     /// A collection of `<iframe>` sizes to send back to script.\\n41:     pub iframe_sizes: Mutex<IFrameSizes>,\\n42: \\n43:     /// An [`ImageResolver`] used for resolving images during box and fragment\\n44:     /// tree construction. Later passed to display list construction.\\n45:     pub image_resolver: Arc<ImageResolver>,\\n46: \\n47:     /// The [`PainterId`] that identifies which `RenderingContext` that this layout targets.\\n48:     pub painter_id: PainterId,\\n49: }\\n50: \\n51: pub enum ResolvedImage<'a> {\\n52:     Gradient(&'a Gradient),\\n53:     // The size is tracked explicitly as image-set images can specify their\\n54:     // natural resolution which affects the final size for raster images.\\n55:     Image {\\n56:         image: CachedImage,\\n57:         size: DeviceSize,\\n58:     },\\n59: }\\n60: \\n61: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\\n62: pub enum ResolveImageError {\\n63:     LoadError,\\n64:     ImagePending,\\n65:     OnlyMetadata,\\n66:     InvalidUrl,\\n67:     MissingNode,\\n68:     ImageMissingFromImageSet,\\n69:     NotImplementedYet,\\n70:     None,\\n71: }\\n72: \\n73: pub(crate) enum LayoutImageCacheResult {\\n74:     Pending,\\n75:     DataAvailable(ImageOrMetadataAvailable),\\n76:     LoadError,\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: pub(crate) struct ImageResolver {\\n80:     /// The origin of the `Document` that this [`ImageResolver`] resolves images for.\\n81:     pub origin: ImmutableOrigin,\\n82: \\n83:     /// Reference to the script thread image cache.\\n84:     pub image_cache: Arc<dyn ImageCache>,\\n85: \\n86:     /// A list of in-progress image loads to be shared with the script thread.\\n87:     pub pending_images: Mutex<Vec<PendingImage>>,\\n88: \\n89:     /// A list of fully loaded vector images that need to be rasterized to a specific\\n90:     /// size determined by layout. This will be shared with the script thread.\\n91:     pub pending_rasterization_images: Mutex<Vec<PendingRasterizationImage>>,\\n92: \\n93:     /// A list of `SVGSVGElement`s encountered during layout that are not\\n94:     /// serialized yet. This is needed to support inline SVGs as they are treated\\n95:     /// as replaced elements and the layout is responsible for triggering the\\n96:     /// network load for the corresponding serialized data: urls (similar to\\n97:     /// background images).\\n98:     pub pending_svg_elements_for_serialization: Mutex<Vec<UntrustedNodeAddress>>,\\n99: \\n100:     /// A shared reference to script's map of DOM nodes with animated images. This is used\\n101:     /// to manage image animations in script and inform the script about newly animating\\n102:     /// nodes.\\n103:     pub animating_images: Arc<RwLock<AnimatingImages>>,\\n104: \\n105:     // A cache that maps image resources used in CSS (e.g as the `url()` value\\n106:     // for `background-image` or `content` property) to the final resolved image data.\\n107:     pub resolved_images_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ServoUrl, CachedImageOrError>>>,\\n108: \\n109:     /// The current animation timeline value used to properly initialize animating images.\\n110:     pub animation_timeline_value: f64,\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: impl Drop for ImageResolver {\\n114:     fn drop(&mut self) {\\n115:         if !std::thread::panicking() {\\n116:             assert!(self.pending_images.lock().is_empty());\\n117:             assert!(self.pending_rasterization_images.lock().is_empty());\\n118:             assert!(\\n119:                 self.pending_svg_elements_for_serialization\\n120:                     .lock()\\n121:                     .is_empty()\\n122:             );\\n123:         }\\n124:     }\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: impl ImageResolver {\\n128:     pub(crate) fn get_or_request_image_or_meta(\\n129:         &self,\\n130:         node: OpaqueNode,\\n131:         url: ServoUrl,\\n132:         destination: LayoutImageDestination,\\n133:     ) -> LayoutImageCacheResult {\\n134:         // Check for available image or start tracking.\\n135:         let cache_result =\\n136:             self.image_cache\\n137:                 .get_cached_image_status(url.clone(), self.origin.clone(), None);\\n138: \\n139:         match cache_result {\\n140:             ImageCacheResult::Available(img_or_meta) => {\\n141:                 LayoutImageCacheResult::DataAvailable(img_or_meta)\\n142:             },\\n143:             // Image has been requested, is still pending. Return no image for this paint loop.\\n144:             // When the image loads it will trigger a reflow and/or repaint.\\n145:             ImageCacheResult::Pending(id) => {\\n146:                 let image = PendingImage {\\n147:                     state: PendingImageState::PendingResponse,\\n148:                     node: node.into(),\\n149:                     id,\\n150:                     origin: self.origin.clone(),\\n151:                     destination,\\n152:                 };\\n153:                 self.pending_images.lock().push(image);\\n154:                 LayoutImageCacheResult::Pending\\n155:             },\\n156:             // Not yet requested - request image or metadata from the cache\\n157:             ImageCacheResult::ReadyForRequest(id) => {\\n158:                 let image = PendingImage {\\n159:                     state: PendingImageState::Unrequested(url),\\n160:                     node: node.into(),\\n161:                     id,\\n162:                     origin: self.origin.clone(),\\n163:                     destination,\\n164:                 };\\n165:                 self.pending_images.lock().push(image);\\n166:                 LayoutImageCacheResult::Pending\\n167:             },\\n168:             // Image failed to load, so just return the same error.\\n169:             ImageCacheResult::FailedToLoadOrDecode => LayoutImageCacheResult::LoadError,\\n170:         }\\n171:     }\\n172: \\n173:     pub(crate) fn handle_animated_image(&self, node: OpaqueNode, image: Arc<RasterImage>) {\\n174:         let mut animating_images = self.animating_images.write();\\n175:         if !image.should_animate() {\\n176:             animating_images.remove(node);\\n177:         } else {\\n178:             animating_images.maybe_insert_or_update(node, image, self.animation_timeline_value);\\n179:         }\\n180:     }\\n181: \\n182:     pub(crate) fn get_cached_image_for_url(\\n183:         &self,\\n184:         node: OpaqueNode,\\n185:         url: ServoUrl,\\n186:         destination: LayoutImageDestination,\\n187:     ) -> Result<CachedImage, ResolveImageError> {\\n188:         if let Some(cached_image) = self.resolved_images_cache.read().get(&url) {\\n189:             return cached_image.clone();\\n190:         }\\n191: \\n192:         let result = self.get_or_request_image_or_meta(node, url.clone(), destination);\\n193:         match result {\\n194:             LayoutImageCacheResult::DataAvailable(img_or_meta) => match img_or_meta {\\n195:                 ImageOrMetadataAvailable::ImageAvailable { image, .. } => {\\n196:                     if let Some(image) = image.as_raster_image() {\\n197:                         self.handle_animated_image(node, image.clone());\\n198:                     }\\n199: \\n200:                     let mut resolved_images_cache = self.resolved_images_cache.write();\\n201:                     resolved_images_cache.insert(url, Ok(image.clone()));\\n202:                     Ok(image)\\n203:                 },\\n204:                 ImageOrMetadataAvailable::MetadataAvailable(..) => {\\n205:                     Result::Err(ResolveImageError::OnlyMetadata)\\n206:                 },\\n207:             },\\n208:             LayoutImageCacheResult::Pending => Result::Err(ResolveImageError::ImagePending),\\n209:             LayoutImageCacheResult::LoadError => {\\n210:                 let error = Err(ResolveImageError::LoadError);\\n211:                 self.resolved_images_cache\\n212:                     .write()\\n213:                     .insert(url, error.clone());\\n214:                 error\\n215:             },\\n216:         }\\n217:     }\\n218: \\n219:     pub(crate) fn rasterize_vector_image(\\n220:         &self,\\n221:         image_id: PendingImageId,\\n222:         size: DeviceIntSize,\\n223:         node: OpaqueNode,\\n224:         svg_id: Option<String>,\\n225:     ) -> Option<RasterImage> {\\n226:         let result = self\\n227:             .image_cache\\n228:             .rasterize_vector_image(image_id, size, svg_id);\\n229:         if result.is_none() {\\n230:             self.pending_rasterization_images\\n231:                 .lock()\\n232:                 .push(PendingRasterizationImage {\\n233:                     id: image_id,\\n234:                     node: node.into(),\\n235:                     size,\\n236:                 });\\n237:         }\\n238:         result\\n239:     }\\n240: \\n241:     pub(crate) fn queue_svg_element_for_serialization(\\n242:         &self,\\n243:         element: ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode<'_>,\\n244:     ) {\\n245:         self.pending_svg_elements_for_serialization\\n246:             .lock()\\n247:             .push(element.opaque().into())\\n248:     }\\n249: \\n250:     pub(crate) fn resolve_image<'a>(\\n251:         &self,\\n252:         node: Option<OpaqueNode>,\\n253:         image: &'a Image,\\n254:     ) -> Result<ResolvedImage<'a>, ResolveImageError> {\\n255:         match image {\\n256:             // TODO: Add support for PaintWorklet and CrossFade rendering.\\n257:             Image::None => Result::Err(ResolveImageError::None),\\n258:             Image::CrossFade(_) => Result::Err(ResolveImageError::NotImplementedYet),\\n259:             Image::PaintWorklet(_) => Result::Err(ResolveImageError::NotImplementedYet),\\n260:             Image::Gradient(gradient) => Ok(ResolvedImage::Gradient(gradient)),\\n261:             Image::Url(image_url) => {\\n262:                 // FIXME: images won\\u2019t always have in intrinsic width or\\n263:                 // height when support for SVG is added, or a WebRender\\n264:                 // `ImageKey`, for that matter.\\n265:                 //\\n266:                 // FIXME: It feels like this should take into account the pseudo\\n267:                 // element and not just the node.\\n268:                 let image_url = image_url.url().ok_or(ResolveImageError::InvalidUrl)?;\\n269:                 let node = node.ok_or(ResolveImageError::MissingNode)?;\\n270:                 let image = self.get_cached_image_for_url(\\n271:                     node,\\n272:                     image_url.clone().into(),\\n273:                     LayoutImageDestination::DisplayListBuilding,\\n274:                 )?;\\n275:                 let metadata = image.metadata();\\n276:                 let size = Size2D::new(metadata.width, metadata.height).to_f32();\\n277:                 Ok(ResolvedImage::Image { image, size })\\n278:             },\\n279:             Image::ImageSet(image_set) => {\\n280:                 image_set\\n281:                     .items\\n282:                     .get(image_set.selected_index)\\n283:                     .ok_or(ResolveImageError::ImageMissingFromImageSet)\\n284:                     .and_then(|image| {\\n285:                         self.resolve_image(node, &image.image)\\n286:                             .map(|info| match info {\\n287:                                 ResolvedImage::Image {\\n288:                                     image: cached_image,\\n289:                                     ..\\n290:                                 } => {\\n291:                                     // From <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images-4/#image-set-notation>:\\n292:                                     // > A <resolution> (optional). This is used to help the UA decide\\n293:                                     // > which <image-set-option> to choose. If the image reference is\\n294:                                     // > for a raster image, it also specifies the image\\u2019s natural\\n295:                                     // > resolution, overriding any other source of data that might\\n296:                                     // > supply a natural resolution.\\n297:                                     let image_metadata = cached_image.metadata();\\n298:                                     let size = if cached_image.as_raster_image().is_some() {\\n299:                                         let scale_factor = image.resolution.dppx();\\n300:                                         Size2D::new(\\n301:                                             image_metadata.width as f32 / scale_factor,\\n302:                                             image_metadata.height as f32 / scale_factor,\\n303:                                         )\\n304:                                     } else {\\n305:                                         Size2D::new(image_metadata.width, image_metadata.height)\\n306:                                             .to_f32()\\n307:                                     };\\n308: \\n309:                                     ResolvedImage::Image {\\n310:                                         image: cached_image,\\n311:                                         size,\\n312:                                     }\\n313:                                 },\\n314:                                 _ => info,\\n315:                             })\\n316:                     })\\n317:             },\\n318:             Image::LightDark(..) => unreachable!(\\\"light-dark() should be disabled\\\"),\\n319:         }\\n320:     }\\n321: }\\n322: \", \"url\": \"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/servo--be6a2f99@dab40a01527870225a72e50a68411f7077f5cb2c/-/blob/components/layout/context.rs\"}, \"isError\": false}"

return null.\\n38:     /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\\n39:     /// for the stylesheet\\u2019s base URL and namespace prefixes.\\n40: \\n52:     pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\\n53:         if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n54:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n55:         } SpecificCSSRule\\n77:         } else {\\n78:             unreachable!()\\n79:         }\\n80:     }\\n81: \\n82:     // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\\n83:     // crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\\n23: \\n24: #[dom_struct]\\n25: pub(crate) struct CSSMediaRule {\\n26:     cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\\n27:     #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \\\"Stylo\\\"]\\n28: &CSSStyleSheet, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>) -> CSSMediaRule {\\n35:         let list = mediarule.rules.clone();\\n36:         CSSMediaRule {\\n37:             cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\\n38:             mediarule: RefCell::new(mediarule),\\n39:             medialist: MutNullableDom::new(None),\\n40:         }\\n41: -> DomRoot<CSSMediaRule> {\\n49:         reflect_dom_object(\\n50:             Box::new(CSSMediaRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, mediarule)),\\n51:             window,\\n52:             can_gc,\\n53:         )\\n54:     }\\n55: \\n56:     fn medialist(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\\n57:         self.medialist.or_init(|| {\\n58:             MediaList::new(\\n59: {\\n79:         self.cssconditionrule\\n80:             .update_rules(mediarule.rules.clone(), guard);\\n81:         if let Some(medialist) = self.medialist.get() {\\n82:             medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\\n83:         }\\n84:         *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\\n85:     }\\n86: }\\n87: \\n88: impl SpecificCSSRule for 138. think @ 2026-03-11T18:35:18.359877

Input

{
  "thought": "Now I understand the architecture. Looking at the existing code:\n\n1. `CSSConditionRule` extends `CSSGroupingRule`  \n2. `CSSMediaRule` and `CSSSupportsRule` extend `CSSConditionRule`\n3. `CSSLayerBlockRule` extends `CSSGroupingRule` directly\n\nFor `CSSContainerRule`, it should extend `CSSConditionRule` (just like CSSMediaRule and CSSSupportsRule) since it's also a conditional rule.\n\nThe `CSSConditionRule.ConditionText()` method currently only handles `CSSMediaRule` and `CSSSupportsRule` - I need to add `CSSContainerRule` there.\n\nNow, I need to understand what `ContainerRule` is in the stylo crate. From the imports in cssrule.rs, it uses `StyleCssRule::Container(s)` where `s` should be an `Arc<ContainerRule>`.\n\nThe task needs to check what `ContainerRule` exports from stylo and what fields it has.\n\nLooking at how the media and supports rules work:\n- `MediaRule` has `media_queries` and `rules`\n- `SupportsRule` has `condition` and `rules`\n- `ContainerRule` probably has `condition` and `rules`\n\nLet me check the Bindings directory to see what DOM interfaces exist for CSSContainerRule.\n"
}

Output

"Your thought has been logged."

self.cssrule.parent_stylesheet()\\n61:     }\\n62: \\n63:     pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\\n64:         self.cssrule.shared_lock()\\n65:     }\\n66: \\n67:     pub(crate) fn update_rules(\\n68:         &self,\\n69:         rules: &Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\\n70:         guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\\n71:     ) {\\n72:         if let Some(rulelist) = self.rulelist.get() {\\n73:             rulelist.update_rules(RulesSource::Rules(rules.clone()), guard);\\n74:         }\\n75:     }\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: impl CSSGroupingRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSGroupingRule {\\n79:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-cssrules>\\n80:     fn CssRules(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<CSSRuleList> {\\n81:         // XXXManishearth self.cssrule.as_specific().ty();\\n89:         let containing_rule_types = CssRuleTypes::from(rule_type);\\n90:         let parse_relative_rule_type = match rule_type {\\n91: index,\\n97:             containing_rule_types,\\n98:             parse_relative_rule_type,\\n99:             can_gc,\\n100:         )\\n101:     }\\n102: \\n103:     /// {
  "thought": "Now I have a clear picture of the full implementation needed. Let me also look at the inheritance hierarchy to understand the DOM bindings generated code:\n\nLooking at `inheritance.rs` to understand how `CSSContainerRule` needs to fit in the inheritance chain.\n\nThe `CSSGroupingRule::rulelist()` function uses `downcast::<CSSConditionRule>()` - and since `CSSContainerRule` would extend `CSSConditionRule`, this would work fine because the downcast to CSSConditionRule would succeed.\n\nNow the key question is: What is the `ContainerRule` type from stylo? Looking at `SupportsRule`:\n- `SupportsRule` has `condition` (the supports condition) and `rules`\n- `ContainerRule` should have `condition` (the container condition) and `rules`\n- The `name` of the container (for `containerName`) would be part of the condition\n\nLet me look at what `ContainerRule` provides. Since the code at `cssrule.rs` has `StyleCssRule::Container(_)`, the Container variant must hold an Arc<ContainerRule>. Let me check how the css rule types are defined and find the ContainerRule struct.\n\nFor servo, the main things I need to implement are:\n\n1. **WebIDL file**: `components/script_bindings/webidls/CSSContainerRule.webidl`\n2. **Rust implementation**: `components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs`\n3. **Update `mod.rs`**: Add the new module\n4. **Update `cssrule.rs`**: Replace `unimplemented!()` with actual implementation\n5. **Update `cssconditionrule.rs`**: Add `CSSContainerRule` to the `ConditionText()` dispatch\n\nFor the inheritance chain, looking at the inheritance macro in dom/bindings, I also need to know the inheritance chain for the generated code.\n\nLet me check how inheritance is registered for existing types.\n"

style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\\n9: use style::stylesheets::{CssRule as StyleCssRule, CssRuleType};\\n10: \\n11: use super::cssfontfacerule::CSSFontFaceRule;\\n12: use super::cssimportrule::CSSImportRule;\\n13: use super::csskeyframerule::CSSKeyframeRule;\\n14: use super::csskeyframesrule::CSSKeyframesRule;\\n15: use super::csslayerblockrule::CSSLayerBlockRule;\\n16: use super::csslayerstatementrule::CSSLayerStatementRule;\\n17: use super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\\n18: use super::cssnamespacerule::CSSNamespaceRule;\\n19: use super::cssnesteddeclarations::CSSNestedDeclarations;\\n20: use super::csspropertyrule::CSSPropertyRule;\\n21: use super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\\n22: use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\\n23: use super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSRuleBinding::CSSRuleMethods;\\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::Reflector;\\n27: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\\n28: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\\n29: use crate::dom::window::Window;\\n30: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\\n31: \\n32: #[dom_struct]\\n33: pub(crate) struct CSSRule {\\n34:     reflector_: Reflector,\\n35:     parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\\n36: \\n37:     /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\\n38:     /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\\n39:     /// for the stylesheet\\u2019s base URL and namespace prefixes.\\n40:     parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: impl CSSRule {\\n44:     pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\\n45:         CSSRule {\\n46:             reflector_: Reflector::new(),\\n47:             parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\\n48:             parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\\n49:         }\\n50:     }\\n51: \\n52:     pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\\n53:         if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n54:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n55:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\\n56:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n57:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\\n58:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n59:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\\n60:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n61:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\\n62:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n63:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\\n64:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n65:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\\n66:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n67:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\\n68:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n69:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\\n70:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n71:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\\n72:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n73:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\\n74:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n75:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\\n76:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n77:         } else {\\n78:             unreachable!()\\n79:         }\\n80:     }\\n81: \\n82:     // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\\n83:     // CSSRule based on which rule it is\\n84:     pub(crate) fn new_specific(\\n85:         window: &Window,\\n86:         parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\\n87:         rule: StyleCssRule,\\n88:         can_gc: CanGc,\\n89:     ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\\n90:         // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\\n91:         match rule {\\n92:             StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\\n93:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n94:             },\\n95:             StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\\n96:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n97:             },\\n98:             StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\\n99:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n100:             },\\n101:             StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n102:             StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n103:             StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\\n104:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n105:             },\\n106:             StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\\n107:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n108:             },\\n109:             StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\\n110:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n111:             },\\n112:             StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\\n113:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n114:             },\\n115:             StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\\n116:             StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n117:             StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n118:             StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\\n119:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n120:             },\\n121:             StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\\n122:                 window,\\n123:                 parent_stylesheet,\\n124:                 s,\\n125:                 can_gc,\\n126:             )),\\n127:             StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n128:             StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\\n129:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n130:             },\\n131:             StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n132:             StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n133:             StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n134:             StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n135:             StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n136:             StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\\n137:                 window,\\n138:                 parent_stylesheet,\\n139:                 s,\\n140:                 can_gc,\\n141:             )),\\n142:         }\\n143:     }\\n144: \\n145:     /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\\n146:     pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\\n147:         self.deparent();\\n148:         // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\\n149:     }\\n150: \\n151:     /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\\n152:     pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\\n153:         self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\\n154:         // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\\n155:         // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\\n156:         // and browsers implement this behavior\\n157:         self.as_specific().deparent_children();\\n158:     }\\n159: \\n160:     pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\\n161:         &self.parent_stylesheet\\n162:     }\\n163: \\n164:     pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\\n165:         self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\\n166:     }\\n167: \\n168:     pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\\n169:         match style_rule {\\n170:             StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\\n171:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\\n172:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n173:                 }\\n174:             },\\n175:             StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\\n176:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n177:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n178:                 }\\n179:             },\\n180:             StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\\n181:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\\n182:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n183:                 }\\n184:             },\\n185:             StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n186:             StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n187:             StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\\n188:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\\n189:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n190:                 }\\n191:             },\\n192:             StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\\n193:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\\n194:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n195:                 }\\n196:             },\\n197:             StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\\n198:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\\n199:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n200:                 }\\n201:             },\\n202:             StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\\n203:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\\n204:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n205:                 }\\n206:             },\\n207:             StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\\n208:             StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n209:             StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n210:             StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\\n211:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\\n212:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n213: rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n218:                 }\\n219:             },\\n220:             StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n221: 166. think @ 2026-03-11T18:38:05.584731

Input

{
  "thought": "I need to figure out what `ContainerRule` looks like in the stylo crate. Let me search for its definition in the servo/servo repository which includes stylo code or in a stylo repo.\n\nActually, stylo is a git dependency. The key is: what does `style::stylesheets::ContainerRule` provide?\n\nLooking at similar code in Firefox/servo, the `ContainerRule` struct should have:\n- `name`: an optional container name (type `ContainerName` which is a `CustomIdent`)\n- `condition`: a `ContainerCondition` (the query part)\n- `rules`: `Arc<Locked<CssRules>>`\n\nFor `containerName` JS property: returns the name or empty string\nFor `conditionText`: returns `name condition_text` or just `condition_text`\n\nI can check the WPT test to understand better:\n```\nassert_equals(r.containerName, name, \"containerName\");\nassert_equals(r.containerQuery, query, \"containerQuery\");\nassert_equals(r.conditionText, name ? `${name} ${query}` : query, \"conditionText\");\n```\n\nSo `containerName` is the name part, `containerQuery` is the query part, and `conditionText` combines both.\n\nNow let me check the stylo crate. The stylo crate is at https://github.com/servo/stylo. Let me search for it on sourcegraph or look for it in the cargo vendor/registry.\n"
}

Output

"Your thought has been logged."

\\\\u003cscript\\\\u003e\\\\n9:   const sheet = style_elm.sheet;\\\\n10: \\\\n11:   function insertRule(source_text) {\\\\n12:     try {\\\\n13:       while (sheet.cssRules.length) {\\\\n14: without query\\\");\\n29: \\n30:   test(() => {\\n31:     const rule = insertRule(\\\"@container (width > 300px) {}\\\");\\n32:     assert_true(!!rule, \\\"Rule successfully parsed\\\");\\n33: 173. think @ 2026-03-11T18:38:39.431360

Input

{
  "thought": "Now I understand the full spec:\n- `containerName`: the optional name of the container query (e.g., \"name\" in `@container name (min-width: 100px)`)\n- `containerQuery`: the query part (e.g., \"(min-width: 100px)\")\n- `conditionText`: combines both - `\"name (min-width: 100px)\"` or just `\"(min-width: 100px)\"` if no name\n\nNow I need to understand the `ContainerRule` struct from stylo. Let me look for what the servo/servo repo exports from the stylo dependency at that version.\n\nLooking at what's already used in the codebase, `StyleCssRule::Container(_)` holds an `Arc<ContainerRule>`. Based on Firefox's implementation and looking at how other rules like SupportsRule work, I can infer:\n\n```rust\npub struct ContainerRule {\n    pub name: Option<ContainerName>,    // or just wrapped custom ident\n    pub condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>,\n    pub rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    pub source_location: SourceLocation,\n}\n```\n\nI need to find the actual type. Let me look at any other files in the servo repo that reference ContainerRule.\n\nActually, looking at the ToCssWithGuard usage, `ContainerRule` implements `ToCssWithGuard`. The key question is: what fields does it have?\n\nLet me search for uses of ContainerRule in the full servo/servo code, not just the sg-evals mirror.\n\nActually, I should check the Cargo.lock to see the exact version pinned, and then find the stylo source.\n"
}

Output

"Your thought has been logged."

Let me look at the servo/stylo GitHub repository via any means available.\n\nAlternatively, looking at the existing servo code for how rules like SupportsRule and MediaRule work, and using the context from the earlier task exploration, I can infer what ContainerRule looks like.\n\nBased on the WPT tests and the spec, ContainerRule should have:\n1. A `name` field: `Option<ContainerName>` where ContainerName is a CSS custom ident wrapper\n2. A `condition` field: Some form of `ContainerCondition` \n3. A `rules` field: `Arc<Locked<CssRules>>`\n\nThe key question is the exact field names and types.\n\nLooking at this commit in stylo, I know that in Firefox's version, `ContainerRule` has:\n- `name: ContainerName` (which is `Atom`)\n- `condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>`\n- `rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>`\n- `source_location: SourceLocation`\n\n`ContainerName` is `Option<Atom>` effectively - an empty atom means no name.\n\nFor serialization (`ToCssWithGuard`), the ContainerRule should serialize as `@container name condition { rules }`.\n\nLet me look at how csssupportsrule.rs works to understand the pattern, and look at cssrule.rs to understand what's currently there.\n"
}

If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\\n3:  * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\\n4: \\n5: use std::cell::Cell;\\n6: \\n7: use dom_struct::dom_struct;\\n8: use style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\\n9: use style::stylesheets::{CssRule as StyleCssRule, CssRuleType};\\n10: \\n11: use super::cssfontfacerule::CSSFontFaceRule;\\n12: use super::cssimportrule::CSSImportRule;\\n13: use super::csskeyframerule::CSSKeyframeRule;\\n14: use super::csskeyframesrule::CSSKeyframesRule;\\n15: use super::csslayerblockrule::CSSLayerBlockRule;\\n16: use super::csslayerstatementrule::CSSLayerStatementRule;\\n17: use super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\\n18: use super::cssnamespacerule::CSSNamespaceRule;\\n19: use super::cssnesteddeclarations::CSSNestedDeclarations;\\n20: use super::csspropertyrule::CSSPropertyRule;\\n21: use super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\\n22: use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\\n23: use super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\\n24: use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSRuleBinding::CSSRuleMethods;\\n25: use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\\n26: use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::Reflector;\\n27: use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\\n28: use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\\n29: use crate::dom::window::Window;\\n30: use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\\n31: \\n32: #[dom_struct]\\n33: pub(crate) struct CSSRule {\\n34:     reflector_: Reflector,\\n35:     parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\\n36: \\n37:     /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\\n38:     /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\\n39:     /// for the stylesheet\\u2019s base URL and namespace prefixes.\\n40:     parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\\n41: }\\n42: \\n43: impl CSSRule {\\n44:     pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\\n45:         CSSRule {\\n46:             reflector_: Reflector::new(),\\n47:             parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\\n48:             parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\\n49:         }\\n50:     }\\n51: \\n52:     pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\\n53:         if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n54:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n55:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\\n56:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n57:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\\n58:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n59:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\\n60:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n61:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\\n62:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n63:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\\n64:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n65:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\\n66:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n67:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\\n68:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n69:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\\n70:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n71:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\\n72:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n73:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\\n74:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n75:         } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\\n76:             rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\\n77:         } else {\\n78:             unreachable!()\\n79:         }\\n80:     }\\n81: \\n82:     // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\\n83:     // CSSRule based on which rule it is\\n84:     pub(crate) fn new_specific(\\n85:         window: &Window,\\n86:         parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\\n87:         rule: StyleCssRule,\\n88:         can_gc: CanGc,\\n89:     ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\\n90:         // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\\n91:         match rule {\\n92:             StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\\n93:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n94:             },\\n95:             StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\\n96:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n97:             },\\n98:             StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\\n99:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n100:             },\\n101:             StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n102:             StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n103:             StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\\n104:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n105:             },\\n106:             StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\\n107:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n108:             },\\n109:             StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\\n110:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n111:             },\\n112:             StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\\n113:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n114:             },\\n115:             StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\\n116:             StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n117:             StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n118:             StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\\n119:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n120:             },\\n121:             StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\\n122:                 window,\\n123:                 parent_stylesheet,\\n124:                 s,\\n125:                 can_gc,\\n126:             )),\\n127:             StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n128:             StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\\n129:                 DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\\n130:             },\\n131:             StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n132:             StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n133:             StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n134:             StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n135:             StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n136:             StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\\n137:                 window,\\n138:                 parent_stylesheet,\\n139:                 s,\\n140:                 can_gc,\\n141:             )),\\n142:         }\\n143:     }\\n144: \\n145:     /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\\n146:     pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\\n147:         self.deparent();\\n148:         // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\\n149:     }\\n150: \\n151:     /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\\n152:     pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\\n153:         self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\\n154:         // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\\n155:         // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\\n156:         // and browsers implement this behavior\\n157:         self.as_specific().deparent_children();\\n158:     }\\n159: \\n160:     pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\\n161:         &self.parent_stylesheet\\n162:     }\\n163: \\n164:     pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\\n165:         self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\\n166:     }\\n167: \\n168:     pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\\n169:         match style_rule {\\n170:             StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\\n171:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\\n172:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n173:                 }\\n174:             },\\n175:             StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\\n176:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n177:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n178:                 }\\n179:             },\\n180:             StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\\n181:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\\n182:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n183:                 }\\n184:             },\\n185:             StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n186:             StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\\n187:             StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\\n188:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\\n189:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n190:                 }\\n191:             },\\n192:             StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\\n193:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\\n194:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n195:                 }\\n196:             },\\n197:             StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\\n198:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\\n199:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n200:                 }\\n201:             },\\n202:             StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\\n203:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\\n204:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n205:                 }\\n206:             },\\n207:             StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\\n208:             StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n209:             StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n210:             StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\\n211:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\\n212:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n213:                 }\\n214:             },\\n215:             StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => {\\n216:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\\n217:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n218:                 }\\n219:             },\\n220:             StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n221:             StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\\n222:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\\n223:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone());\\n224:                 }\\n225:             },\\n226:             StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n227:             StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\\n228:             StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n229:             StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n230:             StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\\n231:             StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => {\\n232:                 if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\\n233:                     rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\\n234:                 }\\n235:             },\\n236:         }\\n237:     }\\n238: }\\n239: \\n240: impl CSSRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSRule {\\n241: https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type for constants > 15\\n245:         // we return 0.\\n246:         if rule_type > 15 { 0 } else { rule_type }\\n247:     }\\n248: \\n249:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-parentstylesheet>\\n250:     fn GetParentStyleSheet(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSStyleSheet>> {\\n251:         if self.parent_stylesheet_removed.get() {\\n252:             None\\n253:         } else {\\n254:             Some(DomRoot::from_ref(&*self.parent_stylesheet))\\n255:         }\\n256:     }\\n257: \\n258:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\\n259:     fn CssText(&self) -> DOMString {\\n260:         self.as_specific().get_css()\\n261:     }\\n262: \\n263:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\\n264:     fn SetCssText(&self, _: DOMString) {\\n265:         // do nothing\\n266:     }\\n267: }\\n268: \\n269: pub(crate) trait SpecificCSSRule {\\n270:     fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType;\\n271:     fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString;\\n272:     /// Remove parentStylesheet from all transitive children\\n273:     fn deparent_children(&self) {\\n274: \\n20: #[dom_struct]\\n21: pub(crate) struct CSSConditionRule {\\n22:     cssgroupingrule: CSSGroupingRule,\\n23:     #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \\\"Stylo\\\"]\\n24:     #[no_trace]\\n25:     rules: RefCell<Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>>,\\n26: }\\n27: \\n28: impl CSSConditionRule {\\n29:     pub(crate) fn new_inherited(\\n30:         parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\\n31:         rules: rules: RefCell::new(rules),\\n36:         }\\n37:     }\\n38: \\n39:     pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\\n40:         self.cssgroupingrule.parent_stylesheet()\\n41: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>> {\\n48:         self.rules.borrow().clone()\\n49:     }\\n50: \\n51:     pub(crate) fn update_rules(\\n52:         &self,\\n53:         rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\\n54:         guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\\n55:     ) {\\n56:         self.cssgroupingrule.update_rules(&rules, guard);\\n57:         *self.rules.borrow_mut() = rules;\\n58:     }\\n59: }\\n60: \\n61: impl CSSConditionRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSConditionRule {\\n62:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-cssconditionrule-conditiontext>\\n63:     fn ConditionText(&self) -> DOMString {\\n64:         if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() }\\n42: \\n43:     pub(crate) fn new(\\n44:         window: &Window,\\n45:         parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\\n46:         mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>,\\n47:         can_gc: CanGc,\\n48:     ) -> DomRoot<CSSMediaRule> {\\n49:         reflect_dom_object(\\n50:             Box::new(CSSMediaRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, mediarule)),\\n51:             window,\\n52:             can_gc,\\n53:         )\\n54:     }\\n55: \\n56:     fn medialist(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\\n57:         self.medialist.or_init(|| {\\n58:             MediaList::new(\\n59:                 self.global().as_window(),\\n60:                 self.cssconditionrule.parent_stylesheet(),\\n61:                 self.mediarule.borrow().media_queries.clone(),\\n62:                 can_gc,\\n63:             )\\n64:         })\\n65:     }\\n66: \\n67:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-cssmediarule-interface>\\n68:     pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\\n69:         let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\\n70:         self.mediarule\\n71:             .borrow()\\n72:             .media_queries\\n73:             .read_with(&guard)\\n74:             .to_css_string()\\n75:             .into()\\n76:     }\\n77: \\n78:     pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\\n79:         self.cssconditionrule\\n80:             .update_rules(mediarule.rules.clone(), guard);\\n81:         if let Some(medialist) = self.medialist.get() {\\n82:             medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\\n83:         }\\n84:         *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\\n85:     }\\n86: }\\n87: \\n88: impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSMediaRule {\\n89:     fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\\n90:         CssRuleType::Media\\n91:     }\\n92: \\n93:     fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\\n94:         let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\\n95:         self.mediarule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\\n96:     }\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: impl CSSMediaRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSMediaRule {\\n100:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-media>\\n101:     fn Media(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\\n102:         self.medialist(can_gc)\\n103:     }\\n104: }\\n105: \", \"url\": rule.clone_rules()\\n45:             } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\\n46:                 rule.ensure_rules()\\n47:             } else {\\n48:
